declining.label: The Decline Is Real
declining.description: Negative 10-year change, negative BLS outlook, high AI exposure. All signals point the same direction.

roles.narrative: Grammarly and AI editors already outperform humans on speed and catch-rate. The role has been in terminal decline for a decade — AI is the final chapter.
Wire copy, breaking news summaries, and routine beat coverage are exactly what LLMs produce. Down 26% in a decade, with no reversal in sight.
Voice synthesis has reached broadcast quality. Traffic, weather, and overnight shifts are being automated. Only live event hosting retains human value.
AI can design, deploy, and analyze surveys faster than human teams. The smallest role in the index, and shrinking fast.

resilient.label: The Resilient
resilient.description: Positive growth, strong outlook, low AI exposure. These roles are growing — and AI is accelerating them.

roles.narrative: The orchestrator can't be automated. Creative vision, talent management, and production decisions require human judgment and relationships that no model can replicate.
AI automates the briefing; humans still run the war room. Crisis response, stakeholder strategy, and organizational reputation require judgment that can't be templated.
More AI output means more need for human curation. The person who defines what should be made — and whether it's good enough — becomes more valuable, not less.
Demand is outpacing automation. Every company wants to be data-driven, and AI is accelerating analysis — but the strategic framing of what to study and why still requires human expertise.

turnaround.label: The Turnaround
turnaround.description: Negative 10-year change, but positive BLS outlook. The bleeding may have stopped — these roles are stabilizing or pivoting.

roles.narrative: Stock photography was gutted by AI, but editorial, event, and portrait photography are stabilizing. Presence and human connection can't be generated.
Physical equipment operation and live event management are durable. After a decade of decline, BLS projects stabilization as live content demand holds.
The only role that declined historically but has the strongest projected growth (+6%). AI is compressing production teams but increasing demand for strategic campaign oversight.
Commodity design (social posts, ad variants) is under serious AI pressure, but BLS still projects modest growth. The role is shifting from execution to brand systems and creative direction.
